texto: You must not suppose that I would like you to profess religion without possessing it. A hypocrite is in my opinion one of the most detestable of beings. my opinion is, that every one should honestly and carefully investigate the Bible; and if he can believe it to be the word of God, to follow its teachings." - Brevet Major Thomas J. Jackson (1 March 1851)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James I. Robertson Jr.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a fe. O trecho “You must not suppose that I would like you to profess religion without possessing it. A hypocrite…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, bible, religion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de religion e stonewall-jackson.
